task.title: Backfill a date range
task.shortdesc: Run a job over historical data for a specific time range.

ul.li: You have a job ID (for example, job_123).
Your job is configured for incremental sync and supports a cursor field.

context.p: Incremental jobs normally sync only the latest changes since the last run. A backfill overrides this behavior and re-syncs all data within a specified time range. Common scenarios include populating a new target table, recovering from data corruption, or onboarding historical data after adding a new mapping.

step.cmd: Create a backfill run specifying the time range.
Monitor the run until it completes.

info.note: Backfills can take significantly longer than incremental runs. Use a higher polling interval (for example, 30–60 seconds) to reduce unnecessary API calls.

result.p: The job has synchronized historical data for the specified range.

link.linktext: Run a job and check status
